name: Principense (Lung'ie)

ancestors.notes: Portuguese superstrate from 16th century colonization of Príncipe island; lexifier.
São Tomé Portuguese and Saotomense creole adstrate: both islands were settled together and maintain close contact; Principense and Saotomense are in the same Gulf of Guinea creole cluster.

notes: Principense (Lung'ie). Portuguese-based Gulf of Guinea creole; spoken on Príncipe island, São Tomé and Príncipe. Critically endangered: ~300–500 speakers, mostly elderly. Part of the Gulf of Guinea creole cluster (with Saotomense/Forro and Angolar on São Tomé). KEY FEATURES: (1) LABIAL-VELAR STOPS: gb=[ɡ͡b] and kp=[k͡p] — direct retentions from Niger-Congo (Edoid/Yoruboid) substrate; highly distinctive. (2) PRENASALIZED STOPS: mb/nd/ng word-initial — West African Bantu/Niger-Congo substrate. (3) NASAL VOWELS: nasality marked by following <n> (an/en/in/on/un). (4) SEVEN-VOWEL SYSTEM: a/e/ɛ/i/o/ɔ/u with both mid vowels phonemic. (5) AFFRICATES: tx=[tʃ], j=[dʒ], x=[ʃ]. (6) TONES: Principense has lexical tone (L vs H), though not conventionally marked in orthography. Language is the subject of active documentation efforts (SIL, SOAS).

sources.author: Maurer, P.
sources.title: Principense: Grammar, Texts, and Vocabulary of the Afro-Portuguese Creole of the Island of Príncipe
sources.notes: Definitive grammar of Principense; full phonological description

sources.title: The Gulf of Guinea creoles: genetic and typological relations
